marketFocus.focusTagline: Warsh Fed Speculation / Metals Crash / Tech Divergence
headlines: Warsh Fed Chair nomination speculation (Bloomberg)—hawkish reaction, yields climb, Asia-Pac stocks lower
Precious metals crash: Gold -4.4%, Silver -6.0% on Trump admin comments
Tech divergence: MSFT -10% (Azure miss) vs META +10.4% (record sales)
Tokyo CPI massive miss (1.5% vs 1.8% exp)—JGB yields collapse, JPY disinflationary pressure
marketFocus.macroContext: Markets caught between tech earnings divergence and Fed policy uncertainty. Warsh nomination speculation fuels hawkish expectations, lifting yields and pressuring equities. Precious metals crash on Trump admin rhetoric, while crude rallies on Iran Strait of Hormuz drill warning. JPY weakens on disinflationary Tokyo CPI miss. USD two-way: strength on risk-off, then trimmed. Focus shifts to French GDP (Friday AM) and US Jobless Claims (Friday PM).

tradingSummary: Risk-off tone dominates as Warsh Fed Chair speculation (hawkish) collides with MSFT cloud growth disappointment. Gold/Silver crash (-4.4%/-6.0%) on Trump comments, crude rallies on Iran geopolitical risk. JPY sold on Tokyo CPI miss (1.5% vs 1.8%), 2Y JGB yield collapses 0.75% from 1.13%. USD two-way: firmer on risk-off, then trimmed. EUR eyes French GDP Friday AM, CAD under pressure from Trump 50% aircraft tariff threat. Tech sector split: MSFT drag vs META strength. Volatility elevated.

redFolderNews.event: French GDP Growth Rate YoY Prel
French GDP Growth Rate QoQ Prel

USD.headline: Warsh speculation lifts yields, but strength trims
USD.summary: Warsh Fed Chair speculation (Bloomberg) triggers hawkish reaction—10Y yields climb, DXY firms on risk-off. But strength trimmed ahead of APAC trade. Widening Nov trade deficit points to slower Q4/Q1 growth. Treasury semi-annual report: no major trading partners manipulated currency. Jobless Claims Friday 14:30—watch for labor market cracks. Two-way action continues 1-3 days.

EUR.headline: French GDP Friday 07:30—key catalyst
EUR.summary: French GDP Friday 07:30 (Exp: 1.2% YoY, 0.2% QoQ)—key catalyst. Import prices declining (disinflationary: -2.3% YoY exp vs -2.7% prev). SAP cloud revenue miss (-15%) drags DAX 40 -1.4%, echoing MSFT tech weakness. ECB rhetoric quiet. EUR/USD under 1.20 technically fragile. Focus: French growth print—beat supports EUR, miss extends weakness. Range-bound 1-3 days pre-data.

GBP.headline: Nationwide Housing Prices Friday 08:00
GBP.summary: Nationwide Housing Prices Friday 08:00—monitoring UK property market health. No BoE speakers, no major data outside housing. GBP tracking broader risk sentiment (weak risk-off tone pressures). Cable technically range-bound. EUR/GBP stable. Focus: housing data—weakness signals consumer stress, strength supports GBP. Neutral 1-3 days, data-dependent.

JPY.headline: Tokyo CPI miss kills BoJ hike bets
JPY.summary: Tokyo CPI massive miss: 1.5% vs 1.8% exp (prev 2.0%), Ex Fresh Food 2.0% vs 2.2%. Disinflationary pressure kills BoJ hike bets. 2Y JGB auction 0.7516% (prev 1.129%)—yields collapse 38bps. JPY sold across the board. Warsh Fed Chair speculation (hawkish USD) adds pressure. Safe-haven bid absent despite risk-off. JPY weakness structural—disinflation narrative dominates 1-3 days.

AUD.headline: Copper strength vs precious metals crash
AUD.summary: No major AUD data Friday. RBA rhetoric quiet post-hold. Copper topped $14k/t (LME, before tech issues), but precious metals crash (Gold -4.4%, Silver -6.0%) weighs on commodity FX sentiment. Iron ore stable. AUD tracking risk appetite—weak risk-off tone pressures. China-UK security dialogue resumption (Xinhua) minor positive for China-linked AUD. Watch US Jobless Claims for risk cues. Neutral 1-3 days.

CAD.headline: Trump 50% aircraft tariff threat escalates
CAD.summary: Trump threatens 50% tariff on Canadian aircraft—escalating trade war rhetoric. Crude rallies (WTI $65.42, Brent $70.71) on Iran Strait of Hormuz drill warning, but CAD fails to benefit (tariff overhang). BoC on hold, Macklem focused on tariff risks. Widening US trade deficit (Nov) signals slower North American growth. CAD vulnerable—tariff narrative trumps oil strength 1-3 days.

CHF.headline: Risk-off safe-haven bid emerges
CHF.summary: Safe-haven bid emerges as risk-off tone dominates (Warsh Fed uncertainty, MSFT disappointment, precious metals crash). CHF benefits despite Gold -4.4%—flight to quality into CHF bonds/cash. SNB rhetoric absent. EUR/CHF pressured. Swiss franc strength structural in elevated volatility environment. Watch French GDP Friday—EUR weakness supports CHF. Bullish 1-3 days.

NZD.headline: Risk-off tone pressures commodity FX
NZD.summary: No major NZD data Friday. RBNZ rhetoric quiet. NZD tracking broader risk sentiment—weak risk-off tone pressures. Dairy prices stable (no GlobalDairyTrade auction this week). China-UK security dialogue resumption minor positive for China-linked NZD. Precious metals crash (Gold -4.4%, Silver -6.0%) weighs on commodity FX. Watch US Jobless Claims for risk cues. Neutral 1-3 days.
